notes: SG allows SSH from 0.0.0.0/0. Health check port 80 blocked in outbound rule — this is why ALB health checks fail intermittently. See INC-003 INC-006 INC-010.
Clone of web-01. Same SG misconfiguration.

RDS instance in PUBLIC subnet. Port 3306 open to 0.0.0.0/0. NCSC Principle 3 failure. UK GDPR breach risk — customer PII in this DB. Multi-AZ disabled. Max connections: 150 (regularly exceeded — see INC-002 INC-005 INC-009).
Bucket policy allows public read (intended for CDN). No server-side encryption. No versioning. See INC-008 INC-014 INC-021 — policy keeps getting reset.
Order receipt storage. No SSE. Broad IAM — 6 engineers have s3:* on this bucket. UK GDPR Article 32 risk.
ALB deployed to single AZ only. No cross-zone load balancing. Falls over when eu-west-2a has issues.
Single VPC with no subnet segmentation. All resources in public subnets. No private subnets. No NAT Gateway. Violates three-tier architecture best practice.
EC2 instances running with AdministratorAccess. Any compromise of the web tier = full AWS account access. NCSC Principle 9 failure.
RDS role also has AdministratorAccess. Not reviewed since initial migration.
Dev leaving end of sprint week 2. Access must be revoked. Console + programmatic access both active.
CloudTrail active but logs not encrypted. S3 bucket for logs has no SSE. ISO 27001 Annex A.12.4 failure.
No Auto Scaling Group configured. Traffic spikes hit fixed-capacity instances. Root cause of TRAFFIC_SPIKE incidents.
Route53 has no health checks configured. DNS failover not possible. Compound failure factor in INC-024.
No CloudWatch dashboards or alarms configured. No alerting on CPU EC2 RDS connections or cost thresholds. Finance cannot explain spend growth.
No AWS Backup plan configured beyond RDS 7-day automated snapshots. No cross-region backup. No tested restore.
